popularStory.description:
BIO:  Chief Justiciar of England under Henry I.

RALPH Basset, son of --- (-after 1124).  O …

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
L1NR-RTJ and L1NT-DZV are not his parents. 

A.      ORIGINS
 1.         RALPH Basset [I], son of --- (-after 1124).  Orderic Vitalis names "Goisfredus de Clintona, Radulfus Basset et Hugo de Bocalanda…" among those who were "de ignobili stirpe" and whom King Henry I raised "de pulvere" (from the dust)[185].  Henry of Huntingdon names "Ralph Basset and his son Richard, justices of all England"[186].  Henry I King of England confirmed property ot Eynsham abbey by charter dated 25 Dec 1109, including the donation of "decimam suam de Strattona" by "Gillebertus Basset" and "decimam suam de una hida de Estelai" by "Radulfus Basset"[187].  "…Radulfo Basset" witnessed the charter dated 1113 granted by Henry I King of England in favour of Thorney abbey[188].

References:

https://fmg.ac/Projects/MedLands/ENGLISHNOBILITYMEDIEVAL3.htm#_Toc21417699 (No Parents Listed.) 
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ralph_Basset (No Parents Listed.) 
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Basset_family - Check under Origins in Wikipedia article regarding origins of the Basset Families.

Ralph Basset (sometimes Bassett; died c. 1127) was a medieval English royal justice during the reign of King Henry I of England. He was a native of Normandy, and may have come to Henry's notice while Henry held land in Normandy prior to becoming king. Basset is first mentioned in documents about 1102, and from then until his death around 1127, he was frequently employed as a royal justice. His son Richard Basset also became a royal judge.

Origins
Basset was a native of Montreuil-au-Houlme near Domfront in Normandy, and possibly came to the notice of King Henry while Henry was count of Domfront during the reign of Henry's older brother King William II of England (1087–1100). Either Basset himself or an earlier person with the same name held lands of Robert d'Oilly that were recorded in Domesday Book as in Buckinghamshire and Hertfordshire. If the Ralph in Domesday was not the same as the judge, the Domesday Ralph may have been the judge's father. Other Bassets in the area were also recorded in Domesday, including a Wiliam and a Richard. It is not clear how or if they were related to Ralph. Ralph the judge held lands in Montreuil that in 1150 were considered to be worth two English manors. He held lands in Wallingford and Colston Basset. The lands in Wallingford were worth 16 and a third knight's fees.

Royal service
The first secure mentions of Basset are in royal charters dating to around 1102, where he appears as a witness. He then appears as a judge in a royal dispute with the sheriff of Yorkshire. Basset is named as one of the commissioners of the Liber Winton, a survey of the landholdings in the city of Winchester which took place at some point between 1103 and 1115, probably close to 1110. From his Norman lands, Basset is recorded as donating lands to the Abbey of Saint-Evroul in 1113. In 1111, Basset took part in the Michaelmas session of the Exchequer, and he continued to take part in financial affairs and can be considered as an early Baron of the Exchequer.

Basset appears as a royal justice in 1116, serving in Huntingdonshire. Basset was noted in the Anglo-Saxon Chronicle entry for 1124 as hanging 44 thieves, during an eyre in Leicestershire. Possibly, Basset's severity was part of an attempt to overawe the under-tenants of the Beaumont twins, one of whom, Waleran, Count of Melun rebelled during 1124. During the period 1110–1127, Basset was one of the leading royal justices, and was described by the medieval chronicler Henry of Huntingdon as one of the "justices of all England". Huntingdon's implication is that Basset's scope was over all of England, not limited to his own locality.

Basset is recorded in the Pipe Roll of 1130 as having performed judicial functions in 11 different shires, even though by this point he was already dead. Basset also served on the informal vice-regency council that assisted Henry's wife and son when the king was out of England. Basset seems to have spent most of his judicial and royal career in England, as he only is a witness on one royal document that was drawn up in Normandy. According to the compiler of the Basset family charters, William Reedy, "there is more evidence for Ralph's service for the king in England" than for any other royal servant who was not clergy. His most active period of royal service was from 1120 to 1130. An older view from historians was that Basset was Chief Justiciar of England is not held by historians currently, nor can the idea that Basset was the head of justices or just the head of the itinerant justices during Henry's reign cannot be determined with any confidence either. It is clear that Basset was employed by the king extensively and probably that the nobleman worked mostly full-time for the king.

Basset's rewards for his royal service included a number of manors. Basset was granted the manor of Mixbury by the king, after the family that held it at the time of Domesday Book died out and it escheated to the king. Other lands held by Basset were probably royal rewards also. One was Quiddenham, which had been held in 1086 by crown. Another was Stoney Stanton, originally held in 1086 by Robert Despenser.

Death and legacy
Basset probably died in 1127, and was certainly dead in 1130. He is said to have taken ill at Northampton, and to have been clothed in a monk's habit while on his deathbed. He was buried in the chapterhouse at Abingdon Abbey, which he left a bequest to. A copy of a charter from Archbishop Theobald of Bec, recorded in a cartulary, records most of Basset's manors. While most of the estates seem to have been held by Basset as a sub-tenant, four of the estates appear to have been held as a tenant-in-chief. These estates were not very valuable, and Basset can not be considered a baron, although his son Richard managed through marriage to become a member of the barons. He held lands in nine counties, perhaps centred in Berkshire.

Basset was the founding member of a dynasty of royal servants who continued to serve the kings of England until around 1250 The medieval writer and chronicler Orderic Vitalis described Basset as one of the new men of King Henry, who "raised them, so to say, from the dust". 
Among Basset's four sons were 
1. Richard Basset, and 
2. Nicholas. Another son was 
3. Ralph who became a cleric. A fourth son was 
4. Turstin, who held land around Wallingford. 
Basset also had daughters, but their names are not known. Only the first initial of his wife's name is known, which was A. Richard received the Norman estates, but not most of the English lands, perhaps because he married an heiress.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Ralph Basset Justicar於大約1075年出生在法國諾門迪奧內蒙崔易爾-奧-爾時，他的父親Thurstan de Banastre de Basset of Drayton19歲，母親Ædgitha Adlfflædasdatter of Sachsen - Saxony18歲。他於1098年與Alice de Buci在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾德雷頓 巴塞特結婚。他們至少是5個兒子的父母。他於1110年在英國被列為royal justice。他去世於1127年11月20日在英國英格蘭奧可福爾瓦爾 本 懷特 霍爾享年53歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭。

nameMeanings.name: Basset
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: from a diminutive of Old French bas(se) ‘short, of low stature’ (see Bas ).
English: variant of   Bassett  , a cognate of 1 above.
In some cases also an altered form of French Besset (see Bessette 2). Compare Bassette 1.

variants.name: Bas
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Mauricio, Abelardo, Florencio, Francisco, Jorge, Luis, Manuel, Pedro, Soledad, Vicente.  Eastern Slavic Dmytro, Liya, Pyotr, Sergey.
Catalan: habitational name from a place so named in the district of La Garrotxa, Catalonia.
Basque: topographic name for someone who lived by a wood, from baso ‘wood, forest’.

nameMeanings.name: Ralph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

variants.name: Ralf
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Ralph Basset Justicar was born about 1075, in Montreuil-au-Houlme, Orne, Normandy, France, his father, Thurstan de Banastre de Basset of Drayton, was 19 and his mother, Ædgitha Adlfflædasdatter of Sachsen - Saxony, was 18. He married Alice de Buci in 1098, in Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons. In 1110, his occupation is listed as royal justice in England. He died on 20 November 1127, in Vale of White Horse, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 53, and was buried in England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Ralph Basset Justicar est né environ 1075, à Montreuil-au-Houlme, Orne, Normandie, France, son père, Thurstan de Banastre de Basset of Drayton, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Ædgitha Adlfflædasdatter of Sachsen - Saxony, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Alice de Buci en 1098, à Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils. En 1110, sa profession de son indiquée est royal justice à Angleterre. Il est décédé le 20 novembre 1127, à Vale of White Horse, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 53 ans, et a été enterré à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Ralph Basset Justicar um 1075 in Montreuil-au-Houlme, Orne, Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thurstan de Banastre de Basset of Drayton 19 und seine Mutter Ædgitha Adlfflædasdatter of Sachsen - Saxony 18. Er hat 1098 in Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom Alice de Buci geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne. 1110 ist sein Beruf als royal justice in England aufgeführt. Er starb am 20. November 1127 im Alter von 53 Jahren in Vale of White Horse, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Ralph Basset Justicar è nato il circa 1075 nel Montreuil-au-Houlme, Orne, Normandie, Francia, il suo padre, Thurstan de Banastre de Basset of Drayton, aveva 19 anni e il suo madre, Ædgitha Adlfflædasdatter of Sachsen - Saxony, aveva 18 anni. Lui sposato Alice de Buci nel 1098, nel Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli. Nel 1110, la sua occupazione è indicata come royal justice nel England. Morì il 20 novembre 1127, nel Vale of White Horse, Oxfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 53 anni, e fu sepolto a Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Ralph Basset Justicarが約1075年にフランスノーマンディオルンモントレウイル-アウ-hオウルメで生まれた時，父Thurstan de Banastre de Basset of Draytonは19歳，母Ædgitha Adlfflædasdatter of Sachsen - Saxonyは18歳であった。1098年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアドレイトン バセットでAlice de Buciと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子の親であった。1110年，イギリスでの職業はroyal justiceと記録されている。彼は1127年11月20日にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアベル オフ ウィト ホールゼで53歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスにて埋葬された。

ko.Abingdon-on-Thames, Vale of White Horse,: 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 발 프 휘트 호르스
ko.lifeSummary: Sir Ralph Basset Justicar(이)가 대략 1075년 프랑스 노먼디 른 몽레우이-오-하에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thurstan de Banastre de Basset of Drayton(은)는 19세였고, 그의 어머니 Ædgitha Adlfflædasdatter of Sachsen - Saxony(은)는 18세였다. 그는 1098년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 드라이턴 배싯에서 Alice de Buci(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 1110년, 그의 직업은 영국에서 royal justice이었다. 그는 1127년 11월 20일 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 발 프 휘트 호르스에서 53세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Ralph Basset Justicar nasceu aproximadamente 1075, em Montreuil-au-Houlme, Orne, Normandie, França, seu pai, Thurstan de Banastre de Basset of Drayton, tinha 19 anos e sua mãe, Ædgitha Adlfflædasdatter of Sachsen - Saxony, tinha 18 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice de Buci em 1098, em Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos. Em 1110, sua profissão aparece como royal justice em Inglaterra. Ele faleceu em 20 de novembro de 1127, em Vale of White Horse, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 53 anos, e foi sepultado em Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Abingdon-on-Thames, Vale of White Horse,: веил оф уит хаурс, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Ralph Basset Justicar родился около 1075 г. в местности монтреуил-ау-хоулм, орн, норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени Thurstan de Banastre de Basset of Drayton было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Ædgitha Adlfflædasdatter of Sachsen - Saxony, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice de Buci в 1098 г. в местности дреитон бассетт, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей. В 1110 году он работал по профессии royal justice в местности Англия. Он умер 20 ноября 1127 г. в местности веил оф уит хаурс, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 53 лет и был похоронен в местности Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Ralph Basset Justicar nació aproximadamente 1075, en Montreuil-au-Houlme, Orne, Normandie, Francia, su papá, Thurstan de Banastre de Basset of Drayton, tenía 19 años y su mamá, Ædgitha Adlfflædasdatter of Sachsen - Saxony, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Alice de Buci en 1098, en Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos. En 1110, su ocupación registrada era royal justice en Inglaterra. Murió el 20 de noviembre de 1127, en Vale of White Horse, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 53 años, y fue sepultado en Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
